accessToken.description: Your TikTok Access Token. Please see TikTok’s [Events API documentation](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1701890979375106) for information on how to generate an access token via the TikTok Ads Manager or API.
accessToken.label: Access Token

pixelCode.description: Your TikTok Pixel ID. Please see TikTok’s [Events API documentation](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1701890979375106) for information on how to find this value.
pixelCode.label: Pixel Code

actions.name: Report Web Event
actions.description: Report Web events directly to TikTok. Data shared can power TikTok solutions like dynamic product ads, custom targeting, campaign optimization and attribution.

fields.label: Event Source
fields.description: The type of events you are uploading through TikTok Events API. Please see TikTok's [Events API documentation](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1701890979375106) for information on how to find this value. If no selection is made 'Web' is assumed.

choices.label: The events took place on your website and are measured by a Pixel Code.
The lead events took place on a CRM system and are tracked by a CRM Event Set ID.

event_source.title: Event Source
event_source.description: The type of events you are uploading through TikTok Events API. Please see TikTok's [Events API documentation](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1701890979375106) for information on how to find this value. If no selection is made 'Web' is assumed.

fields.label: Additional Fields
fields.description: Include fields for travel or vehicle events.

event_spec_type.title: Additional Fields
event_spec_type.description: Include fields for travel or vehicle events.

fields.label: Event Name
fields.description: Conversion event name. Please refer to the "Supported Web Events" section on in TikTok’s [Events API documentation](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1701890979375106) for accepted event names.

event.title: Event Name
event.description: Conversion event name. Please refer to the "Supported Web Events" section on in TikTok’s [Events API documentation](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1701890979375106) for accepted event names.

fields.label: Event ID
fields.description: Any hashed ID that can identify a unique user/session.

event_id.title: Event ID
event_id.description: Any hashed ID that can identify a unique user/session.

fields.label: Event Timestamp
fields.description: Timestamp that the event took place, in ISO 8601 format.

timestamp.title: Event Timestamp
timestamp.description: Timestamp that the event took place, in ISO 8601 format.

fields.label: Phone Number
fields.description: A single phone number or array of phone numbers in E.164 standard format. Segment will hash this value before sending to TikTok. e.g. +14150000000. Segment will hash this value before sending to TikTok.

phone_number.title: Phone Number
phone_number.description: A single phone number or array of phone numbers in E.164 standard format. Segment will hash this value before sending to TikTok. e.g. +14150000000. Segment will hash this value before sending to TikTok.

fields.label: Email
fields.description: A single email address or an array of email addresses. Segment will hash this value before sending to TikTok.

email.title: Email
email.description: A single email address or an array of email addresses. Segment will hash this value before sending to TikTok.

fields.label: First Name
fields.description: The first name of the customer. The name should be in lowercase without any punctuation. Special characters are allowed.

first_name.title: First Name
first_name.description: The first name of the customer. The name should be in lowercase without any punctuation. Special characters are allowed.

fields.label: Last Name
fields.description: The last name of the customer. The name should be in lowercase without any punctuation. Special characters are allowed.

last_name.title: Last Name
last_name.description: The last name of the customer. The name should be in lowercase without any punctuation. Special characters are allowed.

fields.label: Address
fields.description: The address of the customer.

fields.label: Order ID
fields.description: Order ID of the transaction.

fields.label: Shop ID
fields.description: Shop ID of the transaction.

fields.label: External ID
fields.description: Uniquely identifies the user who triggered the conversion event. Segment will hash this value before sending to TikTok. TikTok Conversions Destination supports both string and string[] types for sending external ID(s).

external_id.title: External ID
external_id.description: Uniquely identifies the user who triggered the conversion event. Segment will hash this value before sending to TikTok. TikTok Conversions Destination supports both string and string[] types for sending external ID(s).

fields.label: TikTok Click ID
fields.description: The value of the ttclid used to match website visitor events with TikTok ads. The ttclid is valid for 7 days. See [Set up ttclid](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?rid=4eezrhr6lg4&id=1681728034437121) for details.

ttclid.title: TikTok Click ID
ttclid.description: The value of the ttclid used to match website visitor events with TikTok ads. The ttclid is valid for 7 days. See [Set up ttclid](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?rid=4eezrhr6lg4&id=1681728034437121) for details.

fields.label: TikTok Cookie ID
fields.description: TikTok Cookie ID. If you also use Pixel SDK and have enabled cookies, Pixel SDK automatically saves a unique identifier in the `_ttp` cookie. The value of `_ttp` is used to match website visitor events with TikTok ads. You can extract the value of `_ttp` and attach the value here. To learn more about the `ttp` parameter, refer to [Events API 2.0 - Send TikTok Cookie](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=%201771100936446977) (`_ttp`).

ttp.title: TikTok Cookie ID
ttp.description: TikTok Cookie ID. If you also use Pixel SDK and have enabled cookies, Pixel SDK automatically saves a unique identifier in the `_ttp` cookie. The value of `_ttp` is used to match website visitor events with TikTok ads. You can extract the value of `_ttp` and attach the value here. To learn more about the `ttp` parameter, refer to [Events API 2.0 - Send TikTok Cookie](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=%201771100936446977) (`_ttp`).

fields.label: Locale
fields.description: The BCP 47 language identifier. For reference, refer to the [IETF BCP 47 standardized code](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/bcp/bcp47.txt).

locale.title: Locale
locale.description: The BCP 47 language identifier. For reference, refer to the [IETF BCP 47 standardized code](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/bcp/bcp47.txt).

fields.label: Page URL
fields.description: The page URL where the conversion event took place.

url.title: Page URL
url.description: The page URL where the conversion event took place.

fields.label: Page Referrer
fields.description: The page referrer.

fields.label: IP Address
fields.description: IP address of the browser.

fields.label: User Agent
fields.description: User agent from the user’s device.

user_agent.title: User Agent
user_agent.description: User agent from the user’s device.

fields.label: Contents
fields.description: Related item details for the event.

contents.title: Contents
contents.description: Related item details for the event.

brand.title: Brand
brand.description: Brand name of the product item.

fields.label: Content IDs
fields.description: Product IDs associated with the event, such as SKUs. Do not populate this field if the 'Contents' field is populated. This field accepts a single string value or an array of string values.

content_ids.title: Content IDs
content_ids.description: Product IDs associated with the event, such as SKUs. Do not populate this field if the 'Contents' field is populated. This field accepts a single string value or an array of string values.

fields.label: Number of Items
fields.description: Number of items when checkout was initiated. Used with the InitiateCheckout event.

num_items.title: Number of Items
num_items.description: Number of items when checkout was initiated. Used with the InitiateCheckout event.

fields.label: Content Type
fields.description: Type of the product item. When the `content_id` in the `Contents` field is specified as a `sku_id`, set this field to `product`. When the `content_id` in the `Contents` field is specified as an `item_group_id`, set this field to `product_group`.

content_type.title: Content Type
content_type.description: Type of the product item. When the `content_id` in the `Contents` field is specified as a `sku_id`, set this field to `product`. When the `content_id` in the `Contents` field is specified as an `item_group_id`, set this field to `product_group`.

fields.label: Currency
fields.description: Currency for the value specified as ISO 4217 code.

currency.title: Currency
currency.description: Currency for the value specified as ISO 4217 code.

fields.label: Value
fields.description: Value of the order or items sold.

value.title: Value
value.description: Value of the order or items sold.

fields.label: Description
fields.description: A string description of the web event.

description.title: Description
description.description: A string description of the web event.

fields.label: Query
fields.description: The text string that was searched for.

query.title: Query
query.description: The text string that was searched for.

fields.label: Limited Data Use
fields.description: Use this field to flag an event for limited data processing. TikTok will recognize this parameter as a request for limited data processing, and will limit its processing activities accordingly if the event shared occurred in an eligible location. To learn more about the Limited Data Use feature, refer to [Events API 2.0 - Limited Data Use](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1771101204435970).

limited_data_use.title: Limited Data Use
limited_data_use.description: Use this field to flag an event for limited data processing. TikTok will recognize this parameter as a request for limited data processing, and will limit its processing activities accordingly if the event shared occurred in an eligible location. To learn more about the Limited Data Use feature, refer to [Events API 2.0 - Limited Data Use](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1771101204435970).

fields.label: Test Event Code
fields.description: Use this field to specify that events should be test events rather than actual traffic. You can find your Test Event Code in your TikTok Events Manager under the "Test Event" tab. You'll want to remove your Test Event Code when sending real traffic through this integration.

test_event_code.title: Test Event Code
test_event_code.description: Use this field to specify that events should be test events rather than actual traffic. You can find your Test Event Code in your TikTok Events Manager under the "Test Event" tab. You'll want to remove your Test Event Code when sending real traffic through this integration.

fields.label: Delivery Category
fields.description: Category of the delivery.

choices.label: In Store - Purchase requires customer to enter the store.
Home Delivery - Purchase is delivered to the customer.

fields.label: Prediected Lifetime Value
fields.description: Predicted lifetime value of a subscriber as defined by the advertiser and expressed as an exact value.

predicted_ltv.title: Prediected Lifetime Value
predicted_ltv.description: Predicted lifetime value of a subscriber as defined by the advertiser and expressed as an exact value.

fields.label: Search String
fields.description: The text string entered by the user for the search. Optionally used with the Search event.

search_string.title: Search String
search_string.description: The text string entered by the user for the search. Optionally used with the Search event.

fields.label: CRM Fields
fields.description: Fields related to CRM events.

lead_id.title: TikTok Lead ID
lead_id.description: ID of TikTok leads. Every lead will have its own lead_id when exported from TikTok. This feature is in Beta. Please contact your TikTok representative to inquire regarding availability

lead_event_source.title: TikTok Lead Event Source
lead_event_source.description: Lead source of TikTok leads. Please set this field to the name of your CRM system, such as HubSpot or Salesforce.

fields.label: Vehicle Fields
fields.description: Fields related to vehicle events.

postal_code.title: Postal Code
postal_code.description: Postal code for the vehicle location.

year.title: Year of the Vehicle
year.description: Year the vehicle was laucned in yyyy format.

mileage_value.title: Mileage Value
mileage_value.description: Vehicle mileage (in km or miles). Zero (0) for new vehicle.

mileage_unit.title: Mileage Unit
mileage_unit.description: Mileage unites in miles (MI) or kilometers (KM).

preferred_price_range_min.title: Minimum Preferred Price
preferred_price_range_min.description: Minimum preferred price of the vehicle.

preferred_price_range_max.title: Maximum Preferred Price
preferred_price_range_max.description: Maximum preferred price of the vehicle.

viewcontent_type.title: Soft Lead Landing Page
viewcontent_type.description: Optional for ViewContent. Use viewcontent_type to differentiate between soft lead landing pages.

search_type.title: Other Search Page
search_type.description: Optional for Search. Use search_type to differentiate other user searches (such as dealer lookup) from inventory search.

registration_type.title: Other Registration Page
registration_type.description: Optional for CompleteRegistration. Use registration_type to differentiate between different types of customer registration on websites.

fields.label: Travel Fields
fields.description: Fields related to travel events.

suggested_hotels.title: Suggested Hotels
suggested_hotels.description: Suggested hotels. This can be a single string value or an array of string values.

departing_departure_date.title: Departure Date
departing_departure_date.description: Date of flight departure. Accepted date formats: YYYYMMDD, YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM-DDThh:mmTZD, and YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssTZD

returning_departure_date.title: Arrival Date
returning_departure_date.description: Date of return flight. Accepted date formats: YYYYMMDD, YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM-DDThh:mmTZD, and YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssTZD

destination_ids.title: Destination IDs
destination_ids.description: If a client has a destination catalog, the client can associate one or more destinations in the catalog with a specific flight event. For instance, link a particular route to a nearby museum and a nearby beach, both of which are destinations in the catalog. This field accepts a single string value or an array of string values.

departing_arrival_date.title: Departing Arrival Date
departing_arrival_date.description: The date and time for arrival at the destination of the outbound journey. Accepted date formats: YYYYMMDD, YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM-DDThh:mmTZD, and YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssTZD

returning_arrival_date.title: Returning Arrival Date
returning_arrival_date.description: The date and time when the return journey is completed. Accepted date formats: YYYYMMDD, YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM-DDThh:mmTZD, and YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssTZD

travel_class.title: Flight Ticket Class
travel_class.description: Class of the flight ticket, must be: "eco", "prem", "bus", "first".

user_score.title: User Score
user_score.description: Represents the relative value of this potential customer to advertiser.

preferred_num_stops.title: Preferred Number of Stops
preferred_num_stops.description: The preferred number of stops the user is looking for. 0 for direct flight.

travel_start.title: Start Date of the Trip
travel_start.description: The start date of user's trip. Accept date formats: YYYYMMDD, YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM-DDThh:mmTZD, and YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssTZD.

travel_end.title: End Date of the Trip
travel_end.description: The end date of user's trip. Accept date formats: YYYYMMDD, YYYY-MM-DD, YYYY-MM-DDThh:mmTZD, and YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssTZD.

suggested_destinations.title: Suggested Destination IDs
suggested_destinations.description: A list of IDs representing destination suggestions for this user. This parameter is not applicable for the Search event. This field accepts a single string value or an array of string values.
